title: podman build errors with 'Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 2'
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
"Unable to pass more than one build-arg".  
Is this really true.  This is a  huge limitation for podman if that's the case.  It supposed to accept all docker commands.  e.g. just alias docker to podman, says the documentation.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run this

```
podman build ${IMAGE_NAME}:${SERVICE_BUILD} \
                            -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:${SERVICE_VERSION} \
                            -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:latest \
                            --build-arg ENV=${ENV} \
                            --build-arg DOCKER_REGISTRY=${DOCKER_REGISTRY} \
                            --build-arg REGISTRY_REPOSITORY=${REGISTRY_REPOSITORY} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_NAME=${SERVICE_NAME} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_GROUP=${SERVICE_GROUP} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_BUILD=${SERVICE_BUILD} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_VERSION=${SERVICE_VERSION} \
                            -f ${DOCKERFILE_PATH} .
```
**Describe the results you received:**
obfuscated with *** to protect the innocent.
```
podman build ******.azurecr.io/***.gov/ria/ria-jsonify-svc:0.0.1.8 -t ***.azurecr.io/***.gov/ria/ria-jsonify-svc:0.0.1 -t ***.azurecr.io/***.gov/ria/ria-jsonify-svc:latest --build-arg=ENV=dev --build-arg=DOCKER_REGISTRY=*****.azurecr.io --build-arg=REGISTRY_REPOSITORY=***.gov --build-arg=SERVICE_NAME=ria-jsonify-svc --build-arg=SERVICE_GROUP=ria --build-arg=SERVICE_BUILD=0.0.1.8 --build-arg=SERVICE_VERSION=0.0.1 -f ./docker/Dockerfile .
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 2
```

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
+ podman version
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Wed Sep 23 16:18:02 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

latest version?: I believe I'm putting the latest docker build, from redhat
checked Podman Troubleshooting Guide?: No, but I have been searching google with no luck

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running as a jenkins build agent CICD pod via kubernetes.  Using Azure Kubernetes Service version 1.18.10

comments.body:
Replace with
```
podman build  -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:${SERVICE_BUILD} \
                            -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:${SERVICE_VERSION} \
                            -t ${IMAGE_NAME}:latest \
                            --build-arg ENV=${ENV} \
                            --build-arg DOCKER_REGISTRY=${DOCKER_REGISTRY} \
                            --build-arg REGISTRY_REPOSITORY=${REGISTRY_REPOSITORY} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_NAME=${SERVICE_NAME} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_GROUP=${SERVICE_GROUP} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_BUILD=${SERVICE_BUILD} \
                            --build-arg SERVICE_VERSION=${SERVICE_VERSION} \
                            -f ${DOCKERFILE_PATH} .
```
To be clear - you're missing a `-t` to indicate the name we should tag the image with. `podman build` takes at most one argument, image name is a flag.
thanks that worked.  tricky that the original docker command is able to take that long command without the first -t, but podman requires -t prior to the first image name.

I'm trying to do the something similar:

```env
SRC_URL='https://linktosource'
GIT_USERNAME='my-username'
GIT_EMAIL='my-email@domain.com'
```

This command:

```console
$ cat .env | xargs printf -- '--build-arg %s\n' | xargs podman build . -f file-name -t image:tag
```

ends in

```console
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3
```

What am I missing?

I get the same error with:

```
podman build . $(./buildarg.sh .env) -f dockerfile-dev -t image:tag
```

where `buildarg.sh` is

```sh
#!/bin/bash

awk '{ sub ("\\\\$", " "); printf " --build-arg %s", $0  } END { print ""  }' $@
```

Sources:

1. https://stackoverflow.com/a/43475672/8828460
2. https://stackoverflow.com/a/71088107/8828460


`podman version 4.3.0`

Can you try with the context directory at the end?

podman build $(./buildarg.sh .env) -f dockerfile-dev -t reaper:dev .

Yes, I tried.

```console
$ podman build $(./buildarg.sh .env) -f dockerfile-dev -t image:tag .
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3
```

Didn't help.
